title: Exceptions, Diagnostics, Exit Status, and Recovery · Program Brief: Causal Diagnostics
summary: The command catches Exception around the whole program, prints a traceback to stdout, exits zero after failure, hides the path and cause, and leaves a partial report behind. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the causal diagnostics increment in causal error and recovery contract. Learners progress through predi…
objectives: Write concise actionable diagnostics containing operation and safe context while retaining a debuggable causal chain.
Translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-errors-exit-status-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-errors-exit-status-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_errors_exit_status_4(expected_failures: int = 2, mapped_failures: int = 2, exit_status: int = 2, partial_output: bool = False, recovery_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if expected_failures < 0 or mapped_failures < 0:
        return False, mapped_failures, "failure-count"
    if mapped_failures != expected_failures:
        return False, mapped_failures, "unmapped-error"
    if exit_status == 0:
        return False, exit_status, "false-success"
    if partial_output or not recovery_named:
        return False, exit_status, "unsafe-recovery"
    assert exit_status > 0
    return True, exit_status, "causal-failure"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Write concise actionable diagnostics containing operation and safe context while retaining a debuggable causal chain.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Causal Diagnostics model · Theory Causal Diagnostics, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Causal Diagnostics precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Causal Diagnostics model · Theory Causal Diagnostics, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 7300 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to map expected failures to causal diagnostics, stable nonzero status, atomic output, and recovery guidance; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Write concise actionable diagnostics containing operation and safe context while retaining a debuggable causal chain.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Causal Diagnostics model · Theory Causal Diagnostics, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A full traceback is always the clearest message for end users.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-causal-diagnostics-1a71xf.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-causal-diagnostics-1a71xf(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_causal_diagnostics_1a71xf\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?mapped_failures != expected_failures)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?exit_status == 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?partial_output)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not recovery_named)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using write and concise plus specific case evidence.
